title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin breaks down Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering for software development, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the other 90%. The video positions agentic engineering as the future direction, contrasting it with "vibe coding" and emphasizing the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles for developers.
writeup: Cole Medin breaks down Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, centering on the claim that the AI model drives only 10% of results while the surrounding harness, context engineering, tools, and verification systems account for the other 90%. The analysis maps a spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and describes a shift in developer roles from conductor to orchestrator. It also covers token economics and what those costs imply for software development in 2026.
